mode_selected_reason: Quick-check showed FUSE staleness (data/wins.json, docs/index.html drifted from main) and disk WARNING at 583MB project footprint (above 500MB warn threshold). FUSE staleness diagnosed as the expected universal-pusher rescue lag (decider 09:25Z 403-rescue, workspace-sync 10:06Z will absorb). Disk warning traced to 4 stale /tmp clones totaling 383MB. Schedule-table doc drift was also confirmed and self-fixed via clean clone push.

analyst last 2026-05-05T07:58Z, next 15:57Z (8h cadence on track)
decider last 2026-05-05T09:13Z (5 patches applied, 5 issues closed in run); next 13:13Z (4h cadence)

workspace-sync running on schedule, 10 successful runs since midnight; latest 09:06Z absorbed prior decider commits, next 10:06Z will rescue 09:25Z 403
integrity last 2026-05-05T08:08Z (daily 9 AM cadence on track)
social last 2026-05-04T10:06Z, next 10:05Z today (~25 min from this run)

evidence.fuse_drift: data/wins.json (FUSE 472933B vs GH 472611B, +322B) and docs/index.html (FUSE 1499514B vs GH 1499194B, +320B). Both have FUSE mtime 09:26:27Z, exactly matching decider 09:25Z run. Decider report push_status='403-rescued' / 'Universal-pusher rescue executed — 8 files copied to FUSE'. Expected behavior — workspace-sync 10:06Z will push the rescue files.
evidence.disk: Project-induced footprint 583MB (above 500MB warn threshold, well below 1000MB crit). Breakdown: /sessions/.../mnt 199MB, /tmp/check-clone 127MB (May 4 19:35), /tmp/win001-fix-clone 109MB (May 4 19:36), /tmp/dome-clone 86MB (May 3 23:07), /tmp/tinker-clone 61MB (May 2 06:18). 4 stale clones = 383MB.
evidence.errors_in_agent_outputs: 1 hard 403 (decider's own report — already self-classified as expected and rescued). Soft complaints: PROP-014 Mech 1 still_pending=2 across last 4 days (ISS-1827 closed but verification_pattern_failed; ISS-1834 open with no verification_pattern field). narrative-cite uncited=2995 bogus=15 (acknowledged pre-existing baseline).

previous_followup.claim: Decider's 09:24Z daily-report missing push_status / commit_sha / push_failure_log / recovery_path fields that appeared in 05-24Z report.
previous_followup.verification_method: Surveyed push_status presence across the last 10 decider daily-reports (2026-05-03 through 2026-05-05).
previous_followup.outcome: RESOLVED — not a reporting bug. The push_status field is only emitted by decider when there is an active 403-rescue or pending rescue, not on every run. Evidence: 2026-05-04T13-26 has it (rescue path); 2026-05-04T09-24, 17-20, 21-21, 2026-05-05T01-19, 05-23 all omit it (push succeeded on first try, no rescue triggered); today's 2026-05-05T09-25 has it (another 403). The yesterday-9:24Z FUSE-vs-GH 600B delta I observed was the lagged universal-pusher rescue from a prior failure (e.g. 05:24's 403_rescue_pending), not a fresh undocumented failure. Recommend NO PROP — the emit-on-incident design is reasonable telemetry hygiene.

Today's decider 09:25Z run touched only data/wins.json and other runtime data files (no NEVER_PUSH source-file half) so the original hazard pattern did NOT recur today. Continuing to watch.

previous_followup.verification_method: md5 wins.json FUSE vs GH; survey workspace-sync run logs for 24h.
previous_followup.outcome: FIXED. 06:06Z workspace-sync committed 11 files including data/wins.json + docs/index.html. Subsequent 4 syncs all routine. Yesterday's wins.json rescue absorbed cleanly. (Today's mismatch is a NEW rescue from decider's 09:25Z 403, on track for 10:06Z absorption.)

new_findings.title: Schedule-table doc drift in CLAUDE.md and monitor/prompts/tinker.md
new_findings.evidence: Live cron via list_scheduled_tasks: dome-curmudgeon=4h (cron 30 3,7,11,15,19,23 * * *), dome-decider=4h (cron 10 2,6,10,14,18,22 * * *), dome-workspace-sync=hourly (cron 0 * * * *), dome-analyst=8h (cron 50 */8 * * *). Pre-fix CLAUDE.md table had curmudgeon/decider as '8h quiet' and workspace-sync as 'Every 4h'. Pre-fix tinker.md table had analyst='Every 4h', curmudgeon='Hourly', decider='Every 2h', workspace-sync='Every 4h'. None of these matched live cron.
new_findings.self_fix_applied: commit 7e828b9 on main: 7 line edits across CLAUDE.md and monitor/prompts/tinker.md to match live cron. Verified post-push via GitHub API (raw CDN was still serving cached pre-push content but contents endpoint returns the new tinker.md table).
new_findings.verification_pattern: curl -s -H "Authorization: token $PAT" 'https://api.github.com/repos/funwithscience-org/dome-model-review/contents/monitor/prompts/tinker.md?ref=main' | python3 -c 'import json,sys,base64; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(base64.b64decode(d["content"]).decode())' | grep -E 'Workspace-sync|Decider|Curmudgeon|Analyst' | head -4 — should show 'Every 8h', 'Every 4h', 'Every 4h', 'Hourly'.

new_findings.title: /tmp clone leak — 4 stale ad-hoc clones totaling 383MB
new_findings.evidence: Stale /tmp clones (oldest first): /tmp/tinker-clone (May 2, 61MB) — leftover from a 3-day-old tinker run; /tmp/dome-clone (May 3, 86MB); /tmp/check-clone (May 4 19:35, 127MB); /tmp/win001-fix-clone (May 4 19:36, 109MB). All 4 contain valid dome-model-review checkouts, mtime stable (no active use). Project footprint 583MB pushes the dispatcher's 500MB warn threshold, ~417MB headroom to the 1000MB crit threshold.
new_findings.self_fix_applied: NONE — clones not deleted. Tinker rule says 'Conservative self-fixes. Only fix things you're certain about.' These are ad-hoc clones (not the standard scheduled-agent ephemeral clones, which agents do clean themselves) and may belong to operator debugging sessions or interrupted runs that the operator wants to inspect. Auto-deletion risks losing context. Today's tinker self-fix used /tmp/tinker-runclone-20260505 and was cleaned up immediately after push (good baseline behavior).
new_findings.recommendation: Operator: review /tmp/check-clone, /tmp/dome-clone, /tmp/tinker-clone, /tmp/win001-fix-clone — if no longer needed, rm -rf to free ~383MB. Tinker will keep watching footprint; if it crosses 1000MB without operator action, escalate to medium-severity finding with a PROP for an explicit operator-ack-and-sweep workflow.
new_findings.verification_pattern: du -sm /tmp/check-clone /tmp/dome-clone /tmp/tinker-clone /tmp/win001-fix-clone 2>/dev/null — all four should disappear once operator cleans up; project-footprint should drop below 250MB.

new_findings.title: PROP-014 Mech 1 stuck on 2 issues across multiple days
new_findings.evidence: workspace-sync agent_notes across 2026-05-04 and 2026-05-05 consistently report 'Mech1 still_pending=2 (ISS-1827, ISS-1834 verification_pattern_failed)'. ISS-1827 is in closed-issues.json with status='fixed-pending-verification' (no closed_at). ISS-1834 is in open-issues.json since 2026-05-04T21:18Z (12.4h old) with no verification_pattern field — that's why Mech 1 fails on it.
new_findings.diagnosis: ISS-1834 was authored by curmudgeon (source: WIN-014.c5.json) with no verification_pattern. Decider's issue-creation flow doesn't emit verification_pattern for curmudgeon-sourced issues — the field is added when decider self-applies a patch. ISS-1834 hasn't been patched yet (it's a WIN-012 detail_evidence sign-convention propagation, recommended_action specifies the exact replacement). Once decider patches it, the verification_pattern should flow naturally. ISS-1827 is closed but verification_pattern_failed — would need to inspect what verification_pattern was attached and why it fails.
new_findings.recommendation: Don't author a PROP yet. Wait for decider's 13:13Z run to patch ISS-1834 (decider already has 5 self-applies under its belt this morning). If ISS-1834 stays unpatched 24h+ or both ISS-1827 and ISS-1834 are still pending after a curmudgeon re-review cycle, escalate to PROP for a Mech-1-skip-or-flag mechanism.

Universal-pusher mode block (lines 101-103) is now ~10 days old and stable; could potentially be shortened by trimming the parenthetical history once it's settled, but not urgent.

tinker_self.trend_vs_yesterday: unchanged (no edits to tinker.md beyond the 4-line schedule-table fix)

other_side_considered: Counter-arguments to today's choices: (1) Should I have deleted the 4 stale /tmp clones autonomously? They're not in active use (mtimes >1d) and total 383MB. Counter-counter: tinker rule is conservative self-fixes; auto-deleting an operator's debugging clone (especially win001-fix-clone, which has a topic-specific name suggesting an in-progress investigation) could destroy unrecoverable scratch work. The current footprint is at warn (583MB) not crit (1000MB), so delegating to operator is proportionate. (2) Should I have written a PROP for the schedule-table drift instead of self-fixing? The prompt says 'Conservative self-fixes. Only fix things you're certain about — stale URLs, wrong paths, missing fields.' Schedule values are like wrong paths: factually-incorrect data with a verifiable correct value. The fix is reversible and trivially verifiable. PROP would be over-engineering. (3) Should I have escalated PROP-016 today? It's now ~89h old without operator response. Counter: escalation looks like nagging when operator may be deliberately holding the decision pending more data; the pattern hasn't recurred today (decider's 09:25 run touched only runtime data, not source files). Keeping it on the watch list is the right call. (4) Should FINDING-2026-05-04-001 have been escalated rather than resolved? On revisit, the emit-on-incident pattern is consistent across 10 reports — what looked like a reporting gap yesterday is actually a sensible design choice (don't emit zero-information fields on success). Marking RESOLVED is honest; insisting on the prior framing would be confirmation bias.
